title: Sirloin Steak with Creamy Noodle Stir Fry
description: A comforting and flavorful malaysian-inspired dish featuring sirloin steak and creamy noodles.
instructions: 1. Cook noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
2. In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
3. Add the sirloin steak to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
4. Transfer the steak to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes.
5. In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat.
6. Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
7. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
8. Stir in the soy sauce and cook for 1 minute.
9. Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and stir to combine with the sauce.
10. Add the cream to the skillet and stir to combine with the noodles and sauce.
11. Slice the rested sirloin steak against the grain and add it to the skillet.
12. Stir to combine all the ingredients and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
13. Serve hot, garnished with sesame oil and sesame seeds if desired.
